summary:
The v7.0 system in /Users/dsalamanca/vs_env/NewStrategyFable5 is a two-layer architecture: (1) a numba per-instrument backtest core (`engine/backtest.py::_run_core`) that runs one instrument's 1-minute bid/ask bars against a per-bar `target` array with full IC-Markets-EU-Raw cost realism (spread crossed on every fill, EUR3.25/side commission on FX/metals, reconstructed central-bank policy-rate swaps with broker markups, ESMA leverage, worst-intrabar-mark equity, margin stop-out, negative-balance protection); and (2) a band-book portfolio layer (`mt5/reconcile/gbandrebal/sim.py::run_generic` wrapping `mt5/reconcile/v51_bandharvest.py::_run_window`) that runs 7 equal-capital sleeves as independent sub-accounts on the IC-native bar feed and re-splits the whole book back to 1/N whenever any sleeve slot's share of book equity exceeds 0.25 or falls below (1/7)/1.75≈0.0816 (daily decision, act next midnight, 5-day min gap, k=2.5 harvest backstop kept but subsumed). V7.0 = V6's exact 7 sleeves {BOOK_XAU, BOOK_USTEC, S5_JPY, S1_ETH, ZC_EG, S6_OPEXUSD, BTC_REP} at leverage multiplier R=8 (Python) / R=10 (MT5 live) with this BAND_SYM_25 rule replacing calendar-quarterly rebalancing, plus (MT5-only) H9 delta-resize which adds/reduces same-sign positions at re-splits instead of close+reopen.

Sleeve signals are plain functions `make_signal(bars_df, **params) -> dict(target=np.ndarray[, sl_dist, tp_dist])` where `target` is a per-1m-bar NOTIONAL multiple of the sub-account balance (vol-targeted: vt/realized_vol, clipped at a cap), causal by construction (daily features shift(1), engine additionally executes target[i] at bar i's open, and portfolio_v33.defer_reopen holds targets through 21:00-22:59 UTC). The band-book layer consumes sleeves as `name -> list[(instrument, target_array)]` legs built by `lock_v5.build_sleeves(risk=8.0, CONFIGS['core7sm'], us5_inst='USTEC')` plus the BTC_REP swap in `v52_alternatives.book('BTC_REP','USTEC')`. Every core sleeve factory lives in `strategies/sleeves.py` + `strategies/portfolio_v33.py` (frozen copies), diversifiers in `mt5/reconcile/v5_sleeves.py`, BTC_REP in `mt5/reconcile/v52_alternatives.py::btc_hurdle_legs`.

Official v7.0 numbers come from TWO engines and must never be mixed: the Python band book at R8 on the IC feed 2020-2025 (business-day return accounting, stop_out disabled at 1e-9 via lock_v5 import) gives CAGR 89.72% / bd close-mark DD 15.70% / tick (worst-mark) DD 19.44% / Sharpe 2.58 / final EUR532,230 / 31 band triggers (anchor JSON: mt5/reconcile/gbandrebal/engine_reproduce.json `harvest_band_sym`); the MT5 real-tick Strategy Tester run of PortfolioV7.mq5 at R10, 2020-01→2026-06 crash-inclusive, gives CAGR 96.1% / max equity DD 20.9% Maximal (35.6% Relative/COVID) / Sharpe 2.03 / PF 1.67 / min ML 190%. The full 6-tier Python validation battery is 7/7 hard gates PASS (tier12.json + tier4.json), CPCV 15/15 folds clear the goal (median 85.9%), no-keystone worst = drop-gold at 70.7% CAGR, MC p5 CAGR 63.3%, and EA↔Python reconciliation is `reconciled_with_notes` with 0 unexplained mismatches. Data: `cache/bars_1m_ic/` holds 37 IC-native instruments (bid OHLC + ask=bid+spread reconstruction, minute bars, 2020-01-02→2025-12-31); `cache/bars_1m/` holds 14 Dukascopy tick-derived instruments with the same 8 OHLC columns plus spread_mean/volume; `cache/bars_1m_holdout/` holds 2026-01→2026-05 Dukascopy; `cache/bars_1m_ext/` holds 10 instruments 2015-2025 (v8 work in flight). Environment: Python 3.13.12 (miniforge), numba 0.64.0, pandas 2.3.3, numpy 2.4.2, pyarrow 23.0.1; no requirements file exists.
key_facts: ENGINE _run_core (engine/backtest.py:56-291, @njit(cache=True)): inputs = 8 bid/ask OHLC float64 arrays, eurq (EUR-per-quote-ccy per bar), swap_flag/swap_long/swap_short, target, sl_dist, tp_dist, and scalars contract, comm_side, leverage, lot_step, min_lot, initial, stop_out_level, sizing_mode (0=lots,1=risk,2=notional), margin_cap (default 0.9), rebalance_band (default 0.25), dd_k/dd_floor (DD-responsive de-gross, off by default), throttle_thr/throttle_f (quarter loss throttle, off by default). Returns eq_close, eq_worst, balance, trades[:n_tr] (cols entry_i, exit_i, lots, entry_px, exit_px, pnl_eur, reason 0=signal/1=SL/2=TP/3=stopout), dead flag, per-bar used margin.

Execution semantics: target[i] executes at bar i's OPEN; buys fill at ask_o, sells at bid_o (spread always crossed). Intrabar SL/TP on bid/ask extremes, SL checked FIRST (worst case), gap opens fill at the worse open price. After a forced exit (SL/TP/stopout) re-entry is blocked until target CHANGES (blocked variable).

Notional sizing (mode 2, used by the whole v7 book): lots = balance*|target|*dd_scale*thr_scale/(px*contract*eurq), capped at balance*leverage*margin_cap/unit_eur, floor-rounded to lot_step (0 if < min_lot); engine acts on sign changes OR when held lots deviate from desired by >rebalance_band (25%).

Worst-mark accounting: eq_w[i] = balance + unrealized at bid_l (long) / ask_h (short); eq_c uses bid_c/ask_c. compute_metrics maxdd = max((cummax(eq_c) - eq_w)/cummax(eq_c)). The band engine's mdd_tick instead uses -(eqw/eqw.cummax()-1).min(); its headline CAGR/DD/Sharpe are business-day-resampled close-mark returns annualized at 252/yr (bd_metrics in gbandrebal/sim.py:162-176).

Margin/stop-out: margin = |pos|*contract*mid_close*eurq/leverage; if eq_worst < stop_out_level*margin, force-close at the worst mark. settings.py ACCOUNT stop_out_level=0.50, BUT lock_v5.py:38 sets bt.S.ACCOUNT['stop_out_level']=1e-9 as an import side-effect (the 'noliq' convention: real MT5 magics share one account, per-sleeve liquidation is a modeling artifact). ALL v7 band-book numbers are computed under stop_out=1e-9.

Swap model (engine/costs.py): balance += notional*annual_pct/100/365*day_multiplier*eurq at the first bar at/after 17:00 America/New_York (DST-correct); FX/metals triple Wednesday, indices triple Friday, crypto every calendar day. Rates = central-bank policy-rate step tables (USD/EUR/GBP/JPY/CHF/AUD/NZD/CAD/NOK/SEK, costs.py:39-135) minus markup: FX_MARKUP=1.2%/yr/side (AUDUSD override 2.0), INDEX_MARKUP=4.3%/yr, CRYPTO_SWAP long -20%/yr short 0 (all reconciled 2026-07-03 to the IC MT5 swap sheet). Commission EUR3.25/lot/side FX+metals (settings.py:34), 0 on indices/crypto/energy.

Instrument specs: config/settings.py INSTRUMENTS covers 37 tickers with contract_size, pip, ESMA leverage (30 majors / 20 minors+XAU+indices / 10 XAG+energy / 2 crypto), min_lot 0.01 (0.1 indices). Account: EUR 10,000, margin call 100%, stop-out 50%, negative balance protection.

Sleeve signature: make_signal(bars, **params) -> dict(target=per-1m-bar float array in notional-multiple units[, sl_dist, tp_dist]). Band-book sleeve container: dict name -> list[(instrument, target_array)]; multi-leg sleeves (S6_OPEXUSD 3 FX legs) split one equal-capital slot across legs (legcap = seed*W/len(legs)).

V7.0 sleeve construction: lock_v5.build_sleeves(8.0, CONFIGS['core7sm'], us5_inst='USTEC') gives BASE4 {BOOK_XAU=gold_book_v31(vt=0.55*8), S5_JPY=jpy_smart(vt=0.15*8,cap=20), S1_ETH=crypto_mom(vt=0.40*8,cap=1.2), ZC_EG=eurgbp_zens(vt=0.20*8,cap=20)} via portfolio_v33.build_signals(risk=8) (defer_reopen applied), + BOOK_USTEC=defer_reopen(us500_book_v33(vt=0.85*8)) on USTEC, + S6_OPEXUSD=v5_sleeves.s6_opexusd(risk=8) [long USDJPY/short AUDUSD/short NZDUSD, opex week Mon12:00-Fri20:00 UTC], then v52_alternatives.book('BTC_REP','USTEC') deletes MAG_XAU and adds BTC_REP=btc_hurdle_legs(lb=63, hurdle=0.40, regime=200, vt0=0.40*8, cap=1.2).

BAND_SYM_25 rule (sim.py::first_share_trigger + run_generic): daily close slot-share test; re-split whole book to equal capital when any share > 0.25 (up) or < W/1.75 (down; 0.081633 at N=7, recomputed from enabled count); act next midnight; >=5 days min gap; harvest backstop k=2.5x window-seed slot equity retained (fires 0/31 in Python, 5x in MT5). 31 band triggers over 2020-2025.

Band engine vs per-sleeve engine: run_generic re-runs every [cur,t) segment EXACTLY through _run_window (full close-out costs at every split boundary - the 'no splice flattery' convention); _run_window = each leg run_backtest(sizing='notional', initial=legcap, mask=[lo,hi)), combined via engine/portfolio.combine_curves (close-marks ffilled onto union index; worst marks only on a sleeve's own bars). By contrast engine/portfolio.run_portfolio (old per-sleeve path, PORTFOLIO_FINAL/V33) runs each sleeve once over the full sample with weight*initial and never re-splits; portfolio_v33.run_v33 adds calendar-quarterly re-split over EDGES (2020-01-01 quarterly QS to 2026-01-01).

OFFICIAL Python v7.0 anchor (engine_reproduce.json harvest_band_sym, byte-reproduced by tier12.py): cagr_bd 0.8972225987059659, mdd_bd 0.15703857637905272, mdd_tick 0.1944487798599307, sharpe_bd 2.5804700390982496, final_eq 532229.8433634703, 31 band / 0 harvest triggers. V6 incumbent: 80.34% / 17.31% bd / 20.74% tick / Sharpe 2.35 / final 388,536. No-rebalance counterfactual: 53.5% / 33.7% tick-DD.

MT5 real-tick official (R-frontier reports _32-_37): R10 live = 96.1% CAGR / 20.9% Maximal eq-DD / 35.6% Relative (COVID) DD / Sharpe 2.03 / PF 1.67 / ML 190% / 3 of 24 losing quarters (2020Q1 -21.8%, 2021Q3 -1.4%, 2022Q4 -6.3%) / 0 negative years. R8=86.5%/21.2%; R12=99.8%/21.4% (CAGR knee). MT5-at-R8 retains ~96% of Python CAGR (vs V6's ~65-73%).
Walk-forward per year (Python band book, nothing refit): 2020 +116.5%, 2021 +98.2%, 2022 +52.4% (worst, still clears 50% goal), 2023 +87.4%, 2024 +93.5%, 2025 +124.4%; worst yearly bd-DD 15.7%.

1/N is optimal OOS: expanding-window equal-weight Sharpe 2.32 vs max-Sharpe-refit 1.89; min-var dispersion 0.0pp (min-var IS 1/N). Sleeve P&L shares: XAU ~43%, USTEC ~20%, JPY ~10%, ETH ~9%, ZC_EG ~8%, BTC_REP ~7%, S6 ~4%; ~468 trades/yr, win 58%, PF 1.96.

CPCV library (engine/cpcv.py): N=8 groups, k=2 test, purge 5D, embargo 5D (portfolio variant purge 10D/embargo 5D; SPEC.md amendment raised embargo to 45D for the old v-final line); gate = median OOS path CAGR>50% AND median MaxDD<20% AND >=80% paths profitable; objective = CAGR penalized (DD_BUDGET/maxdd)^2 beyond 20% DD. The v7 battery's CPCV (15 folds, n_blocks=6, k=2, embargo 5bd) was run in gbandrebal/robustness.json, NOT via engine/cpcv.py.

Data cache/bars_1m_ic: 37 parquets named {INST}_IC_1m.parquet; schema bid_o/h/l/c, ask_o/h/l/c (float64), n_ticks (int64), tz-naive-UTC ns timestamp index; built by mt5/reconcile/build_ic_feed.py from MT5 CSV exports (OHLC=bid, ask=bid+spread_points*point), truncated to <2026-01-01. XAUUSD 2,123,532 rows 2020-01-02 01:00 -> 2025-12-31 23:58; USTEC 2,114,245 rows; BTCUSD 2,925,842 rows.

Data cache/bars_1m (Dukascopy, 14 instruments {INST}_2020_2025_1m.parquet): adds spread_mean (float64), volume (float32), n_ticks int32; XAUUSD 2,012,326 rows 2020-01-01 23:00 -> 2025-12-31 21:58. Holdout cache/bars_1m_holdout {INST}_2026H1_1m.parquet runs to 2026-05-31. cache/bars_1m_ext = 2015-2025, 10 instruments (v8 extended-history work).

Feed switching: multifeed_optim.prime_feed('ic'|'duka') clears bt._BARS_CACHE/_PREP_CACHE and injects IC parquets + an ICFx converter (EUR conversion from IC EURUSD/EURJPY/EURGBP/EURNZD/EURCAD/EURCHF/EURNOK/EURSEK mids) into engine.backtest module globals. Engine scripts are single-process by convention (module-level caches).

Environment: /opt/homebrew/Caskroom/miniforge/base/bin/python3 = Python 3.13.12, numba 0.64.0, pandas 2.3.3, numpy 2.4.2, pyarrow 23.0.1, zoneinfo (stdlib). No requirements.txt/pyproject anywhere in the repo. numba @njit(cache=True) compiles on first call (~seconds).

Runtimes observed: gbandrebal/engine_check.py full 9-arm adjudication 4.6 min (single band-book run ~50-100 s); v7val/tier12.py full Tier1+2 battery 8.78 min; v7val/tier4_run.py = 8,000-history block-bootstrap MC (seed 20200101, blocks 20/63/120).

MT5 side: live EA mt5/ea/PortfolioV7.mq5 (magic base 360000, hedging account, decisions logged to portfolio_v7_decisions.csv), preset mt5/presets/V7_CORE7BAND_R10_IC.set (InpRisk=10, InpBandUp=0.25, InpBandDownDiv=1.75, InpBandMinGapDays=5, InpEqualWeight=true, InpUS500=USTEC, InpHarvestK=2.5).

Merge-campaign relevant: mt5/reconcile/h14 + h15 and docs/v7/research/H14_FABLE_IMPORT.md / H15_FABLE2_IMPORT.md already study importing FableMultiAssets sleeves (crisis/carry/mean-rev/seasonal core-seat candidates) into this book; research/fable_ma2_import & fable_ma2_import2 hold the imported code; v7final/reverify.py computes honest min-lot-feasible marginals for those candidates (crisis +3.97pp matched-DD, seasonal +1.27pp, both DD-reducing dilution slots deferred at lam=1).
architecture: Three nested layers. LAYER 1 - per-instrument tick-realistic core: config/settings.py (account + 37 instrument specs) -> engine/loader.py + engine/bars.py (Dukascopy ticks -> 1m bid/ask bars) or mt5/reconcile/build_ic_feed.py (MT5 M1 CSV -> IC bars) -> engine/costs.py (policy-rate swap model, FxConverter, rollover) -> engine/backtest.py (numba _run_core: next-bar-open fills at bid/ask, intrabar SL-first, worst-mark equity, margin stop-out, swaps, 3 sizing modes; python wrapper run_backtest with module-level _BARS_CACHE/_PREP_CACHE/_FX that multifeed_optim.prime_feed hot-swaps between duka and IC feeds). LAYER 2 - signal libraries: strategies/sleeves.py (all edge factories, daily-causal, vol-targeted notional targets), strategies/portfolio_v33.py (frozen v3.3 factories incl. gold_book_v31/us500_book_v33/defer_reopen/build_signals/EDGES quarter grid), mt5/reconcile/v5_sleeves.py (S6_OPEXUSD/VS_GC/MAG_XAU diversifiers), mt5/reconcile/v52_alternatives.py (BTC_REP hurdle momentum + book() assembler). LAYER 3 - portfolio engines, in order of evolution: engine/portfolio.run_portfolio (static sub-account weights, no re-split; PORTFOLIO_FINAL era), portfolio_v33.run_v33 / lock_v5.run (calendar-quarterly equal-capital re-split over EDGES, quarterly seed compounding), and the v7 band engine mt5/reconcile/gbandrebal/sim.run_generic = v51_bandharvest._run_window (equal-capital windows re-run exactly between concentration-band triggers: share>0.25 or <(1/N)/1.75, daily decision, next-midnight action, 5-day min gap, k=2.5 harvest backstop). Validation harnesses sit beside layer 3: engine/cpcv.py + engine/cpcv_portfolio.py (general CPCV library), mt5/reconcile/v7val/tier12.py + tier4_run.py (the v7.0 battery), gbandrebal/engine_check.py (band-vs-calendar adjudication), v7recon/recon.py (EA<->Python reconciliation). The MT5 mirror (PortfolioV7.mq5 + V7_CORE7BAND_R10_IC.set) re-implements the sleeves + band rule + H9 delta-resize natively and is the live/deployment engine.
how_to_run: OFFICIAL v7.0 Python reproduction (headline + no-keystone + walk-forward + 1/N gates): cd /Users/dsalamanca/vs_env/NewStrategyFable5 && python3 mt5/reconcile/v7val/tier12.py  -- ~9 min, prints 'CAGR 89.72% bd-DD 15.70% tick-DD 19.44% Sharpe 2.58 final 532,230 triggers 31' with byte_for_byte=True, writes mt5/reconcile/v7val/tier12.json (script hardcodes absolute repo paths).
Band-vs-calendar adjudication (V6 baseline 80.3%/17.3% + all band arms): python3 mt5/reconcile/gbandrebal/engine_check.py  -- ~4.6 min, writes mt5/reconcile/gbandrebal/engine_reproduce.json.

Tier-4 Monte Carlo + stress: python3 mt5/reconcile/v7val/tier4_run.py  -- 8,000 block-bootstrap histories, writes mt5/reconcile/v7val/tier4.json (gates mc_p5_cagr>40 -> 63.31, P(DD>30)<5% -> 0.475%). NOTE: docs/v7 says 'tier4.py' but the actual file is tier4_run.py.

Minimal programmatic run of the exact deployed config: from repo root, sys.path.insert 'mt5/reconcile/gbandrebal' and 'mt5/reconcile', then: import sim; from sim import run_generic, book, prime_feed, pack, LO, HI, W7; prime_feed('ic'); sl = book('BTC_REP','USTEC'); out, trig = run_generic(sl, [LO,HI], up=0.25, down=W7/1.75, kmult=2.5); m = pack(out, trig)  -- expect m['cagr_bd']=0.897222..., m['n_band']=31. (Importing sim auto-applies stop_out=1e-9 via lock_v5.)
V6 incumbent baseline with per-sleeve P&L attribution: python3 mt5/reconcile/v52_baseline.py (~1-2 min).

Prior locked versions: python3 mt5/reconcile/lock_v4_ustec.py (V4 lock, guardrail asserts); python3 mt5/reconcile/lock_v5.py run core7sm 8 ic (V5.1-style book); python3 mt5/reconcile/v51_rig.py (duka+2026H1 rig with USA500 proxy).
Engine unit tests: python3 tests/test_engine.py and python3 tests/test_sleeves_causality.py.
Rebuild IC parquets after new MT5 exports: python3 mt5/reconcile/build_ic_feed.py (reads MQL5/Files/IC_<sym>_m1.csv from the Wine MT5 install path hardcoded at build_ic_feed.py:16).

MT5 live-config reproduction (the 96.1%/20.9% headline): attach mt5/ea/PortfolioV7.mq5 to any M1 chart in the MT5 Strategy Tester with preset mt5/presets/V7_CORE7BAND_R10_IC.set, hedging account, 2020-01->2026-06 real-tick. Cannot be run from Python.
All engine scripts are single-process (module-level bar caches); run one at a time. First numba call JIT-compiles (~seconds, cached).
official_numbers: Two official engines. (1) Python band book (source-of-truth model): gbandrebal sim.run_generic (= v51_bandharvest._run_window) on the IC-native feed, 2020-01-01 -> 2026-01-01, EUR10k, R=8, stop_out=1e-9, business-day-return accounting -> CAGR 89.72% / bd close-mark MaxDD 15.70% / tick worst-mark MaxDD 19.44% / Sharpe 2.58 / final EUR532,230 / 31 band triggers (anchor in mt5/reconcile/gbandrebal/engine_reproduce.json 'harvest_band_sym', byte-reproduced by v7val/tier12.py). V6 incumbent on the same engine: 80.34% / 17.31% / 20.74% / 2.35 / EUR388,536. (2) MT5 real-tick live config (the deployment headline): PortfolioV7.mq5 + V7_CORE7BAND_R10_IC.set, IC-native costs, crash-inclusive 2020-01 -> 2026-06, R=10 -> CAGR 96.1% / max equity DD 20.9% Maximal (Relative/COVID 35.6%) / Sharpe 2.03 / PF 1.67 / min margin level 190% / 0 negative years / 3 of 24 losing quarters. The R8 Python and R10 MT5 figures are at different leverage and are not directly comparable; matched-R8 MT5 = 86.5%/21.2% (~96% retention of Python CAGR). All figures are in-sample; there is no post-2026 holdout.
pitfalls: Stale docs: README.md and docs/VERSION_HISTORY.md still declare V4-USTEC the current locked strategy; docs/CODE_MAP.md stars lock_v4_ustec.py as source of truth. The actual current lock is V7.0, documented only under docs/v7/. Do not trust the root README for status.

Cost-model doc drift: docs/SPEC.md and the engine/costs.py docstring still say commission EUR2.75/side, FX markup 1%/yr, index markup 2.5%; the CODE (settings.py:34, costs.py:141-146) was reconciled 2026-07-03 to EUR3.25/side, FX 1.2%/yr (AUDUSD 2.0), index 4.3%/yr, crypto long -20%/short 0, USA500 dividend 0. The code is authoritative.

lock_v5.py import side-effect sets ACCOUNT['stop_out_level']=1e-9 globally (sim.py, tier12.py, mc_test etc. all inherit it transitively). Any v7 number re-run WITHOUT this convention will show spurious per-sleeve liquidations. Conversely engine defaults (settings 0.50) apply to raw run_backtest calls.

Two DD conventions and two CAGR conventions coexist: engine compute_metrics uses calendar-year CAGR and maxdd = (cummax(eq_close)-eq_worst)/cummax(eq_close); the band engine reports bd-CAGR (252/yr business-day product), bd-DD (close-mark daily) AND mdd_tick (eqw/eqw.cummax). The 89.7/15.7/19.44 triple mixes these deliberately - always state which basis.

MT5 reports two different DD columns: Maximal (EUR-worst, ~R-invariant ~17-26%) vs Relative (%-worst, scales with R: 20.9% at R5 -> 35.6% at R10 -> ~39% at R12+). Earlier notes claiming 'DD is R-invariant' refer only to the Maximal column; the COVID Relative tail is the real R-dependent risk.

The +1.5pp H9 delta-resize benefit exists only in MT5; the validated Python 89.7% band book close-and-reseeds at every trigger. The EA also fires ~2x more band triggers than the Python sim (57 vs 27 at R10) because it triggers on realized slot equity - rule identical, path differs.

v7final/v7_configs.json 'deployable_recommendation' recommends C2cfg = band + fixed-min-lot overlay ring (95.9%/14.7% unlevered) - but the LOCKED V7.0 per docs/v7/STRATEGY.md is C1 (core7+band only, no overlay). The overlay, crisis and seasonal slots were evaluated and deferred. Do not treat C2cfg as shipped.

Band-width sensitivity is the weak axis: tick-DD breaches the 20% goal at up=0.28; ship up<=0.25 strictly. Also at R5 the band is WORSE than V6 (down-band buys laggards into the Feb-2026 gold crash); the band's edge is high-R DD-capping only. The 5-day min-gap is load-bearing and must be exact.

The 0.0816 down-band floor is (1/N)/1.75 computed from the ENABLED sleeve count at runtime, with S6's three legs aggregated into ONE slot before the share test (7 slots, not 9). Hardcoding 0.0816 or iterating S6 legs separately misfires the rule.

USTEC exists only on the IC feed (no Dukascopy cross-check); the duka/2026H1 rig (v51_rig) substitutes USA500 as a documented proxy (corr .89). The 2026H1 event numbers therefore come from the USA500-proxy book, not the deployed USTEC book.

mt5/reconcile scripts have hard-coded sys.path relative-parent inserts and are kept FLAT on purpose - moving them breaks imports; tier12.py/engine_check.py additionally hardcode the absolute repo path /Users/dsalamanca/vs_env/NewStrategyFable5.

Engine module-level caches (_BARS_CACHE/_PREP_CACHE/_FX) mean prime_feed() must be called before signals are built, and only one feed can be live per process; scripts are single-process by convention.

The graveyard is large and documented: DD-throttles/de-risking overlays, weight optimization, gold selectivity filters, instrument expansion, and 6 new strategy families all failed adversarial validation (docs/REGISTRY.md, VERSION_HISTORY.md) - the book was repeatedly shown to sit on its DD-constrained efficient frontier; only R and the re-split cadence moved CAGR-at-DD.

v8 work is in flight (docs/v8/, mt5/reconcile/v8/, cache/bars_1m_ext 2015-2025, research dirs v71-v76 with failed/deferred variants: crisis tail, overlay, VIX gate, carrier hunt). V7.0 is the last CLOSED version; anything under v8/v7x research dirs is unvalidated.

SPEC.md's validation protocol (CPCV embargo 45D amendment, 20% DD gate) applies to the old Dukascopy line; the v7 battery used its own harness (gbandrebal robustness: 6 blocks, k=2, embargo 5bd; hard gates CAGR>50%/DD<30%) - the two protocols differ, cite the right one.
docs/v7/STRATEGY.md §8 tells you to run 'tier4.py'; the actual file is mt5/reconcile/v7val/tier4_run.py.
